Tagging Details.Summary: Authorizes taking areas under special protection
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details: The purpose of this law is to regulate relations concerning the utilization of and taking areas under special protection, preservation, and protection of natural landscape to keep particular features of natural zones and belts, their peculiar formation, forms of rare and rarest fauna and flora, historical and cultural sites, and natural sightseeing, as well as studying and identifying their evolution.

Tagging Details.Summary: Establishes 'National Parks' protected area category
Tagging Details.Toc: Article 3. Classification of Special Protected Areas
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details:
1. State special protected area shall be classified as follows:

[...]

2) National Parks

Tagging Details.Summary: Requires establishment of National Park Restricted Zone
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details:
National Parks shall be divided in respect of their natural state, location of animals and plants, requirement of conservation of historical and cultural monuments, conditions for developing tours and tourism into the following zones:

[...]

3) restricted zone

Tagging Details.Summary: Use weapons in self-defense
Tagging Details.Toc: Article 32. Use of Weapons by Wildlife Rangers
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details:
1. The types of weapons and instruments to be used by wildlife rangers working in the special protected areas and the procedures for their use shall be established by the state central administrative body in consultation with the General Police Office.

2. Wildlife rangers may use weapons in the following events while performing their duties:

1) when the life and health of wildlife rangers are potentially endangered due to a real circumstance;

2) when the life and health of wildlife rangers are potentially endangered by wild animals.

Tagging Details.Summary: Intervene in observed violations
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details:
2. Wildlife rangers working in the special protected areas shall exercise the following power:

[...]

1) to monitor the implementation of this law and other environmental protection legislation, to intervene in the violations detected, and to impose the proper liabilities on guilty persons;

Tagging Details.Summary: Temporarily halt illegal and environmentally harmful activities
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details:
2. Wildlife rangers working in the special protected areas shall exercise the following power:

[...]

4) to stop the activities of citizens, business entities, and organizations conducting activities that have negative impacts on the environment and violate the legislation and protection regime, temporarily, to give them timed orders and instructions and to make demands;

Tagging Details.Summary: Exercise the rights and duties of the state environmental inspectors
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details: 1. Administration staff responsible for environmental protection and wildlife rangers shall have the rights and duties of the state environmental inspectors.

Tagging Details.Summary: Monitor the implementation of all environmental protection laws
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details:
2. Wildlife rangers working in the special protected areas shall exercise the following power:

[...]

1) to monitor the implementation of this law and other environmental protection legislation, to intervene in the violations detected, and to impose the proper liabilities on guilty persons;

Tagging Details.Summary: Enter and audit businesses to monitor the implementation of the law

2. Wildlife rangers working in the special protected areas shall exercise the following power:

2) to enter and audit business entities and organizations to monitor the implementation of the legislation;

Tagging Details.Summary: Search and seize documents, vehicles, weapons, equipment, and contraband
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details:
2. Wildlife rangers working in the special protected areas shall exercise the following power:

Tagging Details.Summary: Create conditions where flora and fauna will grow and reproduce

Tagging Details.Law: Regulation on Use of Springs in Protected Areas
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details: 15. Foreign legal entities, foreign citizens, international organizations, and non-state persons shall be prohibited from using the spring for services in the SPA.

Tagging Details.Summary: Hunting or trapping in a protected area to manage disease outbreaks without guidelines or agreement
Tagging Details.Law: Decree on Approval of Procedure - Infectious Disease in Protected Areas
Tagging Details.Toc: Article 4. Responsibilities of researchers, business entities and organizations for hunting and trapping animals
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details: 17. Researchers, business entities and organizations engaged in hunting and trapping activities in protected areas shall have guidelines approved in accordance with relevant regulations, and shall conduct hunting and trapping activities on the basis of an agreement with the PA Protected Area Administration and relevant soum governors.

Tagging Details.Summary: Selling timber in a protected area without a certificate of origin
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details:
1. Whoever harvests timber from a forest without a permit, or harvests, transports, sells, or purchases timber or timber products without a certificate of origin, shall be punished by a fine in the amount of four hundred and fifty to five thousand four hundred tugriks, or by community service for a period of two hundred and forty to seven hundred and twenty hours, or by restriction of travel rights for a period of one month to one year.

[...]

2. This crime is committed:

[...]

2.3. in forests of specially protected areas of the state;

[...]

by restriction of travel rights for a period of one to five years, or by imprisonment for a period of one to five years.

Penalties Details.Other Details: Restriction of travel rights for 1 - 5 years

Tagging Details.Summary: Violating protected area laws, administrative norms, standards, regimes and procedures
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details:
1. In special protected areas:

1.1. Violation of laws, administrative norms, standards, regimes and procedures;

1.2. A person shall be fined in the amount of three thousand units togrogs and a legal entity shall be fined in the amount of thirty thousand units togrogs for damages and compensation for activities not included in the management plan.

Tagging Details.Summary: Carrying out activities prohibited in Special Protected Areas
Tagging Details.Toc: Article 7.17. Carrying out prohibited activities in special protected areas
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details: 2. Carrying out activities prohibited by law in a specially protected area shall be punishable by a fine of 500 togrogs for a person or 5,000 togrogs for a legal person.

Tagging Details.Summary: Unauthorized entry into a protected area
Tagging Details.Toc: Article 8.1. Violation of the Land Law
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details: 1. Unauthorized entry into a specially protected area, such as installing signs prohibiting access to other people's possession or use, or erecting a fence, shall be punishable by a fine of 25 units togrogs for a person or two hundred and fifty units togrogs for a legal person.

Tagging Details.Summary: Repeated violations of contract terms
Tagging Details.Law: Regulation on Temporary Land Management in Protected Areas
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details: 6.1 Citizens, business entities, and organizations using land in special protected areas shall strictly fulfill their obligations specified in the agreement. Failure to comply with the contract will result in liability under applicable law, and, in the event of repeated violations causing significant environmental damage, the contract will be terminated.

Tagging Details.Summary: Hunting without a permit in a protected area
Tagging Details.Primary Tag Details: 1. Anyone who hunts or captures animals without a permit in a specially protected area, or who hunts or captures rare animals without a permit, intentionally breeds, stores, sells, purchases, transports, exports to foreign countries, or collects stuffed animals, tissues, organs, or raw materials derived from them, shall be punished by a fine of five thousand four hundred to twenty-seven thousand tugriks, or restriction of travel rights for a period of one to five years, or imprisonment for a period of one to five years.

Penalties Details.Other Details: Restriction of travel rights for a period of one to five years
